4147			 * key attributes.  Note that tupnatts will only have been
4148			 * explicitly represented in !heapkeyspace indexes that happen to
4149			 * have non-key attributes.
4150			 */
4151			if (!heapkeyspace)
4152				return tupnatts == nkeyatts;
4153
4154			/* Use generic heapkeyspace pivot tuple handling */
4155		}
4156	}
4157
4158	/* Handle heapkeyspace pivot tuples (excluding minus infinity items) */
4159	Assert(heapkeyspace);
4160
4161	/*
4162	 * Explicit representation of the number of attributes is mandatory with
4163	 * heapkeyspace index pivot tuples, regardless of whether or not there are
4164	 * non-key attributes.
4165	 */
4166	if (!BTreeTupleIsPivot(itup))
4167		return false;
4168
4169	/* Pivot tuple should not use posting list representation (redundant) */
4170	if (BTreeTupleIsPosting(itup))
4171		return false;
4172
4173	/*
4174	 * Heap TID is a tiebreaker key attribute, so it cannot be untruncated
4175	 * when any other key attribute is truncated
4176	 */
4177	if (BTreeTupleGetHeapTID(itup) != NULL && tupnatts != nkeyatts)
4178		return false;
4179
4180	/*
4181	 * Pivot tuple must have at least one untruncated key attribute (minus
4182	 * infinity pivot tuples are the only exception).  Pivot tuples can never
4183	 * represent that there is a value present for a key attribute that
4184	 * exceeds pg_index.indnkeyatts for the index.
4185	 */
4186	return tupnatts > 0 && tupnatts <= nkeyatts;
4187}
4188
4189/*
4190 *
4191 *  _bt_check_third_page() -- check whether tuple fits on a btree page at all.
4192 *
4193 * We actually need to be able to fit three items on every page, so restrict
4194 * any one item to 1/3 the per-page available space.  Note that itemsz should
4195 * not include the ItemId overhead.
4196 *
4197 * It might be useful to apply TOAST methods rather than throw an error here.
4198 * Using out of line storage would break assumptions made by suffix truncation
4199 * and by contrib/amcheck, though.
4200 */
4201void
4202_bt_check_third_page(Relation rel, Relation heap, bool needheaptidspace,
4203					 Page page, IndexTuple newtup)
4204{
4205	Size		itemsz;
4206	BTPageOpaque opaque;
4207
4208	itemsz = MAXALIGN(IndexTupleSize(newtup));
4209
4210	/* Double check item size against limit */
4211	if (itemsz <= BTMaxItemSize)
4212		return;
4213
4214	/*
4215	 * Tuple is probably too large to fit on page, but it's possible that the
4216	 * index uses version 2 or version 3, or that page is an internal page, in
4217	 * which case a slightly higher limit applies.
4218	 */
4219	if (!needheaptidspace && itemsz <= BTMaxItemSizeNoHeapTid)
4220		return;
4221
4222	/*
4223	 * Internal page insertions cannot fail here, because that would mean that
4224	 * an earlier leaf level insertion that should have failed didn't
4225	 */
4226	opaque = BTPageGetOpaque(page);
4227	if (!P_ISLEAF(opaque))
4228		elog(ERROR, "cannot insert oversized tuple of size %zu on internal page of index \"%s\"",
4229			 itemsz, RelationGetRelationName(rel));
4230
4231	ereport(ERROR,
4232			(errcode(ERRCODE_PROGRAM_LIMIT_EXCEEDED),
4233			 errmsg("index row size %zu exceeds btree version %u maximum %zu for index \"%s\"",
4234					itemsz,
4235					needheaptidspace ? BTREE_VERSION : BTREE_NOVAC_VERSION,
4236					needheaptidspace ? BTMaxItemSize : BTMaxItemSizeNoHeapTid,
4237					RelationGetRelationName(rel)),
4238			 errdetail("Index row references tuple (%u,%u) in relation \"%s\".",
4239					   ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(BTreeTupleGetHeapTID(newtup)),
4240					   ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(BTreeTupleGetHeapTID(newtup)),
4241					   RelationGetRelationName(heap)),
4242			 errhint("Values larger than 1/3 of a buffer page cannot be indexed.\n"
4243					 "Consider a function index of an MD5 hash of the value, "
4244					 "or use full text indexing."),
4245			 errtableconstraint(heap, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
4246}
4247
4248/*
4249 * Are all attributes in rel "equality is image equality" attributes?
4250 *
4251 * We use each attribute's BTEQUALIMAGE_PROC opclass procedure.  If any
4252 * opclass either lacks a BTEQUALIMAGE_PROC procedure or returns false, we
4253 * return false; otherwise we return true.
4254 *
4255 * Returned boolean value is stored in index metapage during index builds.
4256 * Deduplication can only be used when we return true.
4257 */
4258bool
4259_bt_allequalimage(Relation rel, bool debugmessage)
4260{
4261	bool		allequalimage = true;
4262
4263	/* INCLUDE indexes can never support deduplication */
4264	if (IndexRelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel) !=
4265		IndexRelationGetNumberOfKeyAttributes(rel))
4266		return false;
4267
4268	for (int i = 0; i < IndexRelationGetNumberOfKeyAttributes(rel); i++)
4269	{
4270		Oid			opfamily = rel->rd_opfamily[i];
4271		Oid			opcintype = rel->rd_opcintype[i];
4272		Oid			collation = rel->rd_indcollation[i];
4273		Oid			equalimageproc;
4274
4275		equalimageproc = get_opfamily_proc(opfamily, opcintype, opcintype,
4276										   BTEQUALIMAGE_PROC);
4277
4278		/*
4279		 * If there is no BTEQUALIMAGE_PROC then deduplication is assumed to
4280		 * be unsafe.  Otherwise, actually call proc and see what it says.
4281		 */
4282		if (!OidIsValid(equalimageproc) ||
4283			!DatumGetBool(OidFunctionCall1Coll(equalimageproc, collation,
4284											   ObjectIdGetDatum(opcintype))))
4285		{
4286			allequalimage = false;
4287			break;
4288		}
4289	}
4290
4291	if (debugmessage)
4292	{
4293		if (allequalimage)
4294			elog(DEBUG1, "index \"%s\" can safely use deduplication",
4295				 RelationGetRelationName(rel));
4296		else
4297			elog(DEBUG1, "index \"%s\" cannot use deduplication",
4298				 RelationGetRelationName(rel));
4299	}
4300
4301	return allequalimage;
4302}
